title: Observation of OAM non-conservation in entangled photon generation
abstract: Orbital angular momentum (OAM)-entangled states produced by spontaneous parametric down-conversion (SPDC) are considered ideal for realizing high-dimensional entangled states, which have several advantages for quantum technologies. However, the limited sensitivity of current two-photon OAM detectors is a major roadblock not only for realizing such technologies but also for resolving foundational questions, such as OAM conservation in SPDC. The current theoretical understanding is that OAM is not conserved in Type-II SPDC but is conserved in Type-I. Experimentally, although non-conservation in TypeII has not been demonstrated, conservation in Type-I has been reported frequently and has become an underlying assumption for techniques generating high-dimensional OAM entangled states. In this work, we experimentally demonstrate a high-sensitivity two-photon OAM detector, using which, contrary to the current understanding, we report non-conservation of OAM in Type-I SPDC. We attribute this to a spatial walk-off effect and prove it using a framework free of standard phase-matching approximations.
summary: This paper reports the experimental observation of Orbital Angular Momentum (OAM) non-conservation in Type-I Spontaneous Parametric Down-Conversion. By developing a high-sensitivity two-photon detector, the authors show that the spatial walk-off effect in BBO crystals causes a spread in the OAM distribution, contrary to the established belief that OAM is conserved in Type-I processes. The study provides a numerical framework to validate these findings and demonstrates that previous claims of conservation were likely due to detector limitations or thin-crystal regimes. This discovery has significant implications for the precision and design of high-dimensional quantum optical technologies.
main_problem: The paper investigates the long-standing controversy regarding whether Orbital Angular Momentum (OAM) is conserved during Type-I Spontaneous Parametric Down-Conversion (SPDC).
main_result: The authors experimentally demonstrate OAM non-conservation in Type-I SPDC, attributing the effect to the spatial walk-off of the pump beam in the nonlinear crystal.
method: The researchers developed a high-sensitivity two-photon OAM detector using a Mach-Zehnder-type interferometer with image rotators and reconstructed the OAM spectrum via a two-dimensional Fourier transform of coincidence visibility.
model_or_system: The physical system is a Type-I degenerate SPDC process in a 15-mm thick BBO (beta-barium borate) nonlinear crystal, utilizing a 405 nm continuous-wave UV pump laser.
key_observables: The two-photon OAM spectrum (joint OAM distribution) $P_{l_s, l_i}$, the non-conservation parameter $N$, and coincidence visibility $V_{si}(	heta_s, 	heta_i)$.
important_parameters: Crystal thickness ($L$), spatial walk-off angle ($\alpha_p$), phase-matching angle ($	heta_p$), and the OAM/radial mode indices ($l, p$).
main_assumptions: The study challenges the standard assumption of OAM conservation ($l_p = l_s + l_i$) in Type-I SPDC and uses a framework that avoids standard phase-matching approximations.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows numerical plots of OAM distribution spread due to crystal thickness and a conceptual diagram of pump walk-off; Figure 2 shows the increase of non-conservation $N$ with crystal thickness and its independence from the phase-matching angle; Figure 7 and 8 show coincidence counts vs. time delay and the signature of temporal HOM interference.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the OAM conservation controversy, describes the development of a new high-sensitivity detector, presents the experimental setup and OAM spectrum reconstruction method, details the experimental results demonstrating non-conservation, and provides a numerical validation and supplementary technical derivations.
why_interesting: This is highly relevant to quantum optics and photonic quantum information as it challenges a fundamental assumption used in generating high-dimensional entangled states and provides a new high-fidelity method for measuring complex spatial modes.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
